channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Assessing the Sustainability of Electric Vehicles Amid Climate Challenges
item.description: Electric Vehicles (EVs) are increasingly promoted worldwide as a solution to reduce carbon emissions and combat climate change. However, the reality is more complicated than the clean image they proje...

item.title: P. C. George contests from Poonjar again in 2026 Kerala Assembly elections
item.description: P. C. George, a veteran Kerala politician and seven-time MLA from the Poonjar constituency, is contesting in the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections. Having served in the Kerala Legislative Assembly for ov...

item.title: Ben Mwonyonyo Gains Attention as Potential Running Mate for Nairobi Governor in 2027
item.description: As the 2027 Nairobi gubernatorial race approaches, Westlands constituency has become a key political battleground. Tim Wanyonyi, the incumbent MP, has been a significant player, with his personal assi...

item.title: West End residents raise concerns over new McDonald’s development
item.description: Construction of a new McDonald’s at 220 Melbourne Street, bordering West End, is set to begin next month, sparking concern among local residents. Brisbane City Council approved the fitout, which will ...

item.title: SAG-AFTRA and Studios Reach Preliminary Agreement to Prevent Strikes
item.description: SAG-AFTRA, the union representing actors and other entertainment professionals, has reached a tentative agreement with major studios in an attempt to avoid potential strikes. The deal, which was negot...

item.title: President Kagame Highlights Tanzania as Vital Trade Partner for Rwanda During Dar es Salaam Visit
item.description: Rwandan President Paul Kagame has visited Dar es Salaam and strongly reaffirmed the close and important partnership between Rwanda and Tanzania. He described Tanzania as a key strategic partner that s...

item.title: Bilawal Commemorates Pakistan's Resilience and Victory in Marka-i-Haq Conflict
item.description: On May 4, 2026, PPP Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari praised Pakistan's resilience during the Marka-i-Haq conflict with India, emphasising that the nation never bows under pressure. Speaking at a comme...

item.title: Robert Wolk and Category Winners Shine at 2026 Simola Hillclimb
item.description: The 16th edition of the Simola Hillclimb in 2026 delivered thrilling motorsport action over a high-energy weekend, with thousands of fans turning out in near-perfect weather conditions. Robert Wolk em...

item.title: Opposition Parties Face Risk of Divisions Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: As Nigeria dey waka towards 2027 general elections, plenty signs dey show say di opposition fit fall into di same waka wey dem waka for 2023. Back then, big political players like Peter Obi, Atiku Abu...

item.title: Protests in the Dominican Republic target proposed Canadian mining development over environmental concerns
item.description: Hundreds of people in the Dominican Republic have taken to the streets to protest a proposed mining project backed by a Canadian company, raising concerns about environmental damage and community impa...

item.title: Babu Owino Hints at Leaving ODM, Announces Plans to Vie for Nairobi Governor
item.description: Embakasi East MP, Babu Owino, has hinted at breaking away from the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) party ahead of the 2027 General Election. Speaking in an interview on May 4, 2026, Owino disclosed p...

item.title: President Ruto Congratulates Kevin Kiarie for Gold Medal Victory at Africa Inline Freestyle Championships
item.description: President William Ruto has finally congratulated Kenyan skating champion Kevin Kiarie after the young athlete won gold at the Africa Inline Freestyle Championships held in Egypt last Saturday. In his ...

item.title: Abuja Cleric Urges Wike to Keep Jabi Lake Recreation Area Open to Public
item.description: Sarah Omakwu, Senior Pastor for Family Worship Centre for Abuja, don give heartfelt appeal to Nyesom Wike, Minister of Federal Capital Territory (FCT), make e no allow Jabi Lake Recreation Area go pri...

item.title: CIT CEO Misled Board Over Misconduct Findings, Inquiry Concludes
item.description: An inquiry into the recruitment of Margot McNeill as CEO of the Canberra Institute of Technology (CIT) has found that she 'actively misled' the board by failing to disclose a misconduct finding made a...

item.title: FG Begins Evacuation of Nigerians from South Africa Amid Xenophobic Tensions
item.description: The Nigerian government has started plans to evacuate citizens from South Africa due to growing fears of xenophobic attacks, particularly in Pretoria and Johannesburg. The Minister of Foreign Affairs,...

item.title: Patient Experiences of Medical Neglect and Gaslighting in the Emergency Room
item.description: The article highlights the experiences of numerous patients who reported being mistreated or dismissed by emergency room staff during medical emergencies. Many shared stories of being accused of exagg...

item.title: Murdoch-linked MacLeod family gains influence in climate-focused political funding
item.description: The article examines the rising political and environmental influence of the MacLeod family, who are connected to the Murdoch media dynasty through Prudence Murdoch and her husband Alasdair MacLeod. I...

item.title: Investigations Continue as No Arrests Are Made in Kubheka Kidnapping
item.description: Gauteng police have confirmed that there have been no arrests in the case of Mazwi Kubheka, the Vosloorus spaza shop owner who was kidnapped and held for a month. Police Commissioner Tommy Mthombeni s...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens digital skills and technology partnership with China
item.description: Tanzania has shown strong commitment to deepen its technological cooperation with China as part of advancing the country’s digital transformation agenda. Speaking during the Experience China High-Tech...

item.title: Residents Allowed to Return After Fatal House Explosion in Bristol
item.description: Residents in a Bristol neighbourhood have been allowed to return to their homes following a fatal explosion that left two people dead and three others injured. The incident occurred at a property on S...
